text: There is an uncanny quiet here, accentuated by the starkness of artificial light and cold glass. I sense the presence of function and purpose—rooms earmarked for activity, yet so thoroughly emptied. The darkness weighs on me slightly, urging philosophical questions: is a place still what it is when stripped of people and intention? My steps echo in the silence, making me hyper-aware of my own existence amid these vast, empty corridors. I feel both present and peripheral, almost as if observing the world behind a thin, transparent veil.
